title: Solution Architect
description:
**Be the change Europe needs! Join Safespring as a Solution Architect!** 




  





 Why this role matters?
 



  





 We’re building the cloud Europe deserves - open, trustworthy, and competitive. The market and opportunity for EU-made tech has never been better.
 



 Our platform runs heavily on open-source and scales from a single VM in public cloud to multiple racks in a customer’s private cloud. Customers are showing up with serious workloads they want to move onto sovereign infrastructure. We need someone who can actually understand what they’re trying to do, then turn that into something engineering can build.
 



  





 That person is
 **you.** 




  





 You’re a translator. You bridge customers and our engineering teams, and translate in both directions. You can sit with a customer’s principal engineer, figure out what they’re really worried about, not the surface ask, the actual concern underneath, and then walk into our infra team and explain it without losing what matters in either direction.
 



  





 You’ve worked on cloud infrastructure or platform products before, ideally somewhere data sovereignty and compliance actually mattered: a sovereign or private cloud vendor, EU public sector, a regulated SaaS, or similar.
 



  





 You can draft a solution architecture and defend it. This isn’t customer-success-with-a-technical-veneer. You have the depth to design and own the design.
 



  





 You write well. Most of our engineering team will only ever encounter the customer through your writing, so clear documentation is half the job.
 



  





 You speak Swedish and English fluently.
 



  





 You can work fully remotely from anywhere in the EU, or from our Stockholm office. We’ll sort you out with the gear you need, plus the stability of a collective bargaining agreement (or equivalent outside Sweden).
 



  





**What you’ll do** 




  




* Run discovery sessions and architecture workshops with customers until you understand their workload deeply enough to design for it.
* Write technical briefs engineering can act on: what’s needed, why, what’s negotiable, what isn’t.
* Draft solution architectures and reference designs. Walk customers through them. Iterate until they fit.
* Translate engineering tradeoffs into decisions customers can make. (“A in three weeks or B in three months” - you can explain what that actually means for their roadmap.)
* Pitch in on delivery when something hands-on is needed.



  





**What you’ll need** 




  





 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺 𝗳𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗹𝘀: Linux at depth, plus Kubernetes, virtualisation, and open infrastructure stacks (OpenStack, Ceph).
 



 𝗔𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 & 𝗱𝗲𝗹𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘆: Ansible and Terraform, CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar), and scripting in Python, Go, or shell.
 



 𝗖𝗹𝗼𝘂𝗱 𝗻𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 & 𝘀𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆: VPCs, load balancers, DNS, firewalls, access controls, audit logging.
 



 𝗢𝗯𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗮𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenSearch.
 



 𝗟𝗮𝗻𝗴𝘂𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀: Fluent in Swedish and English, written and spoken.
 



  





**Above all, drive and curiosity: someone who picks things up, figures them out, and keeps moving, even when the path isn’t fully mapped.** 




  





**Your team** 




  





 You’ll join a small and dense team to start: the CROO, you, and a cloud engineer. You report to the CROO. You work daily with the dev and infra engineering leads. No direct reports.
 



  





 We’re a remote-friendly team, but we value time together. Company offsites and team travel to conferences (KubeCon, OpenInfra, that kind of thing).
 



  





**Reality check** 




  





 Working at a small company means living with ambiguity. Customers don’t arrive with neat requirements. Priorities shift. You’ll regularly choose between “perfect” and “shipped.”
 



 If you want narrow responsibilities or a large specialist team around you, this isn’t the right fit. If you want to own the gap between customer and engineering end to end, you’ll thrive here.
 



  





**About Safespring** 




  





 At Safespring, we’re giving European organizations an alternative to the hyperscalers. Powered by open source our sustainable and European-based cloud infrastructure puts security, transparency, and digital sovereignty first.
 



  





 Questions about this role? Send a message to Marietta, our friendly people lead at marietta@safespring.com.
 



  





 Location: please, notice that for this role you will need to be based in European Union.
 



  





 As we provide services to some of the public research institutions we will conduct background and reference checks as part of the recruitment process for candidates who get offer of employment with us.
 



  





**Starting date:** 




 As soon as possible. Rolling interviews.
 



**Salary range:** 




 70 000 – 90 0000 EUR annually
 



  





 𝘗𝘪𝘤𝘬 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘳𝘪𝘨𝘩𝘵 𝘴𝘪𝘥𝘦 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘴𝘰𝘷𝘦𝘳𝘦𝘪𝘨𝘯 𝘤𝘭𝘰𝘶𝘥 𝘴𝘵𝘰𝘳𝘺. 𝘑𝘰𝘪𝘯 𝘶𝘴!

title: Digital Workplace IT Operations Manager
description:
**Company Description**
 At Sobi, each person brings their unique talents to work as a team and make a difference. We are dedicated to developing and delivering innovative therapies to improve the lives of people who live with a rare disease. Our edge comes from our team of people and our commitment to patients.
   

  

 Here At Sobi, Our Mission And Culture Get Us Excited To Come To Work Every Day, But Here Are a Few More Reasons To Join Our Team
   

  

* Competitive compensation for your work
* Emphasis on work/life balance
* Collaborative and team-oriented environment
* Opportunities for professional growth
* Diversity and Inclusion
* Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments


**Job Description**
 The IT department, a part of IS/IT, are responsible for architecture, strategy, maintenance and support of SOBIs Microsoft platforms, infrastructure such as clients, servers, network. The IT department consist of five teams – Infrastructure, Service desk, IT security, Digital Workplace & AI and Automation & Integration. IT work according to Sobi’s quality framework based on ITIL.
   

  

 As a Digital Workplace IT Operations Manager for Device and Applications you will work in our Microsoft cloud environment. Managing devices and applications in our Windows client and iOS environment, to ensure security, compliance, and productivity. You will enroll and configure devices in Intune, deploy and update applications, monitor health and performance and troubleshoot issues across Windows and iOS.
   

  

**Main Responsibilities**
* Ownership of key elements in the Digital Workplace team’s responsibility
* Lead and coordinate Service requests, Changes and roadmap activities within your area
* Manage and coordinate Digital Workplace incidents in ServiceNow
* Suggest and deliver continuous improvement regarding the individuals appointed systems/products/solutions with a focus on identifying opportunities for process improvement
* Support in managing and coordinating training of ServiceDesk and other departments of Sobi regarding the individuals appointed systems/products/solutions
* Serve as SME for the Digital Workplace & AI, including, but not limited to device management, Intune, RoboPack, applications and trainings
* Application and OS Lifecycle management (Intunes/RoboPack/Windows Autopatch), Apple device/Phone OS and Application Lifecycle Management (Intune/Apple Business Manager)
* Knowledge sharing inside the Team to build a stronger resilience, to protect against interruptions in our delivery


**Qualifications**
* Relevant education within IT or equivalent practical experience
* Previous experience of working as a part of a device team in a Microsoft cloud environment (Intune), ability to create and package applications and configurations in Intune, good knowledge in trouble shooting Windows OS, Windows application and iOS application, good knowledge in Microsoft Autopilot and Autopatch, basic to intermediate PowerShell scripting.
* Previous experience of application management in Robopack, or similar solution
* Ideally, intermediate knowledge about security and compliance related to device management in a global company setting.
* Team player, flexible and adoptable. Pro-active and solution oriented.
* Fluent in Swedish and English

title: Customer Success Engineer - Team lead
description:
Grow with us
   

  

 Upsales was founded in 2003, grew without a single outside investor, and has been profitable ever since. No VC pressure. No compromises. Just a product customers keep paying for.
   

  

 We build the leading CRM and AI platform for B2B sales, and we're now in our highest growth phase in years, one of the very few SaaS companies growing profitably while staying genuinely ahead on AI.
   

  

 If you want real autonomy, colleagues who push you, and work that moves the needle from day one, you'll fit right in.
   

  

**AI first mindset**
 We’re not looking for someone who simply uses AI. We want someone who weaponizes it. You treat tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI copilots as force multipliers, allowing you to deliver at a level and speed others can’t match. For you, AI isn’t an add-on, it’s the default operating system for how you think, work, and lead. You’re relentlessly curious about new capabilities, and you push the limits of what’s possible, turning AI into a competitive advantage for yourself, Upsales, and our customers. This is a role for someone with real ambition: someone who wants to set a new standard for efficiency, precision, and impact in the age of AI.
   

  

**About The Role**
 This is a role for someone who thrives at the intersection of technical depth and customer obsession. As Customer Success Engineer – Team Lead, you'll take ownership of how our customers experience, adopt, and succeed with the Upsales platform — not just by solving problems, but by building the systems, processes, and team culture that make great support scalable and proactive.
   

  

 You'll lead a small CS team while staying hands-on yourself — troubleshooting technical issues, guiding customers toward better solutions, and collaborating closely with Product and Engineering to continuously improve the platform and the customer journey.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Handle and resolve complex technical support cases, and lead the team in doing the same with consistency and quality
* Troubleshoot across system logic, data flows, integrations, and APIs
* Build scalable processes, documentation, and self-service resources
* Work proactively with monitoring and alerting to identify and address issues before they escalate
* Collect and synthesize customer feedback, insights, and patterns to inform product decisions
* Collaborate with Product and Engineering to improve the platform based on real customer needs
* Lead and develop the CS team — building a culture of ownership, curiosity, and genuine care for customer outcomes
* Drive the adoption and development of AI tools within the team — identifying where automation and AI can improve efficiency, response quality, and proactive support, and helping the team build new ways of working around them


**Who You Are**
* Technical background in systems engineering, computer science, or similar
* Experience in technical support or customer success engineering in a SaaS environment
* Proven experience leading a team
* A systems thinker who builds processes, not just fixes problems
* Strong analytical skills and comfortable working with data
* Genuinely excited about AI and how it can transform customer success
* Experience with SQL, APIs, or JavaScript is a plus
* Fluent in both Swedish and English


 Upsales was founded in 2003, grew without a single outside investor, and has been profitable ever since. No VC pressure. No compromises. Just a product customers keep paying for.
   

  

 We're now in our highest growth phase in years, one of the very few SaaS companies growing profitably while staying genuinely ahead on AI.
   

  

 If you want real autonomy, colleagues who push you, and work that moves the needle from day one, you'll fit right in.

title: Projektledare inom IT-infrastruktur- och infrastrukturområdet till Stockholm
description:
*Vill du också arbeta i en miljö där du blir en kugge i en samhällsviktig verksamhet? Vill du påverka och bidra till Sveriges säkerhet och att vi kan fortsätta leva i ett demokratiskt land?*
*I Försvarsmakten gör alla våra medarbetare skillnad för Sverige varje dag* 
 .
   

  

**Om Enheten**
 Försvarsmaktens telekommunikations- och informationssystemförband (FMTIS) har ett samlat ansvar för drift, underhåll och förvaltning av alla ledningsstödsystem i Försvarsmakten. FMTIS verkar kontinuerligt i fred, kris och krig vilket innebär att förbandet är ständigt insatt såväl nationellt som internationellt. Ledningsstödsenheten (LSE) är en del av FMTIS och vi söker nu efter en projektledare till enhetens ledningsgrupp för att stärka vårt team med placering i Stockholm.
   

  

 Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Du kommer som projektledare inom IT-infrastruktur- och infrastrukturområdet på enheten att vara sammanhållande för dessa områden i enhetens planerade, pågående och avslutade projekt. Vid nyetableringar i nya lokaler är det du som projektledare tillser att kunds behov och krav avseende ledningsstödsystem tillgodoses.
   

  

 Du kommer också att ansvara för att avveckla ledningsstödsystem när etableringar lämnas av FM. Du kommer också att vara enhetens representant i arbetet med att utveckla och skapa robusthetshöjande åtgärder i befintliga transmissionsnät och IT-infrastruktur, då i huvudsak inom Stockholmsområdet.
   

  

 Vidare kommer din roll vara att stödja enhetens avdelningar i framdrift av deras lokalt pågående projekt inom området.
   

  

 Du arbetar som projektledare i team tillsammans med andra enheter och avdelningar från olika organisationsenheter i FM, och i nära samverkan med andra myndigheter, främst Försvarets Materielverk och Fortifikationsverket. Försvarsmakten är i stark tillväxt så arbetsuppgifterna kan komma att utvecklas över tid.
   

  

 KRAV
   

  

**Kvalifikationer**
* Gymnasieutbildning med godkända betyg
* God kännedom om Försvarsmaktens ledningars behov av ledningsstödsystem och infrastruktur för att möjliggöra stöd till ledning
* Kännedom om Försvarets telenät (FTN) och dess telekominfrastruktur
* God kommunikativ förmåga i tal och skrift både i svenska och engelska
* B-körkort


**Personliga egenskaper**
 Vi söker dig som är säkerhetsmedveten, har en lösningsorienterad inställning, är engagerad och vill utvecklas i din roll.
   

  

 Du är en god lagkamrat samtidigt som du gärna tar initiativ och eget ansvar. Som person är du trygg, har god pedagogisk förmåga och är lyhörd.
   

  

 I perioder bedrivs arbetet med hög intensitet och under tidspress, vilket ställer krav på din förmåga att arbeta effektivt och prioritera utan att göra avkall på kvalitet. Du har hög ansvarskänsla, god förmåga att arbeta såväl i grupp som självständigt.
   

  

 Stor vikt kommer att läggas vid personlig lämplighet.
   

  

 Meriterande
   

  

* Eftergymnasial utbildning eller erfarenhet inhämtad på annat sätt som arbetsgivaren bedömer likvärdigt
* God kännedom i funktion och uppbyggnad av FM IT-infrastruktur i Stockholmsområdet
* Projektledarutbildning


**Övrigt**
 Befattningen är en tillsvidareanställning och inleds med 6 månaders provanställning för dig som inte är anställd i Försvarsmakten.
   

  

 Arbetsort Stockholm. Befattningen är civil och på heltid.
   

  

 Befattningen medför tjänsteresor både över dagen men också under längre perioder.
   

  

 Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.
   

  

 Upplysningar om befattningen
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om befattningen och rekryteringsprocessen är du välkommen att kontakta chef Marcus Sävensjö
   

  

 För upplysningar angående anställningsvillkor kontakta Hanna Ottosson
   

  

**Fackliga företrädare**
 SACO Agnetha Landquist
   

  

 OFR/S Martin Sparr
   

  

 SEKO-F Matz Felix
   

  

 OFR/O Heikki Videll
   

  

 Samtliga nås via växeln på 08-788 75 00
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan
 **senast 2026-05-29** 
 .
   

  

 Du bifogar ditt CV samt motiverar varför du är intresserad och vad du kan tillföra tjänsten.
   

  

 ,
 **Ansökningar till denna befattning kommer endast tas emot via Försvarsmaktens webbplats.** 
 -------------------------------------------------------- Information om Försvarsmakten och det rekryterande förbandet: Försvarsmaktens telekommunikations- och informationssystemförband, FMTIS, säkerställer Försvarsmaktens förmåga att kommunicera och leda - dygnet runt, året om. Förbandet har ett samlat ansvar för Försvarsmaktens tekniska ledningssystem som stödjer militära insatser till sjöss, på land och i luften – i Sverige och utomlands. Förbandet har närmare 2000 medarbetare på mer än 32 orter runt om i landet. Läs mer på https://jobb.forsvarsmakten.se/fmtis En anställning hos oss innebär placering i säkerhetsklass. Vanligtvis krävs svenskt medborgarskap. Säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll kommer att genomföras före anställning enligt 3 kap i säkerhetsskyddslagen. Med anställning följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras samt att tjänstgöra utomlands. Innebörden av detta varierar beroende på typ av befattning. Till ansökan om anställning ska CV och personligt brev bifogas. Om du går vidare i anställningsprocessen ska alltid vidimerade kopior av betyg och intyg uppvisas. Samtal från externa rekryteringsföretag och säljare undanbedes.

title: AI Product Manager
description:
We are on a mission to become the leading international force in residential property management. What we do matters to billions of people in their everyday lives.
   

  

 Residential real estate is the world’s largest asset class, and for most families, buying a home is the single biggest financial decision they will ever make. For too long, residential property management has fallen short of homeowners' and residents' expectations, and the industry is ready for change. Odevo was formed to make that change happen.
   

  

 In just six years, Odevo has grown more than 70-fold, establishing us as the leading international player. We are more than 12,000 people across the US, the UK, the Nordics, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Mexico, and Italy building Odevo. Together, we aspire to break new ground through innovation and cultivate a workplace where we help each other succeed, where ideas matter more than titles, and where kindness is a strength.
   

  

 By combining the power of people and technology, we set a new standard for how homes are managed. Our technology-enabled services simplify living for residents and create a better experience for property owners and boards.
   

  

 Our ambition is to continue our fast-growing journey through new customers, service expansion, and by partnering with great companies that share our mindset and ambition, both in existing and new markets.
   

  

 As we continue to grow, we stay true to who we are, challenge unnecessary bureaucracy, and keep decision-making close to our customers.
   

  

 If you share our mindset and ambition, we’d love you to join us and help accelerate our mission together.
   

  

 At Odevo, we build production-grade AI systems that change how residential living is managed at scale. Our AI Engineering team is deeply technical, highly collaborative, and driven by learning - and we're now looking for a Product Manager to help shape what we build and why.
   

  

 This is a role at the intersection of cutting-edge AI and real-world property management. You'll work side by side with AI engineers, domain experts, and business stakeholders to turn emerging AI capabilities into products that thousands of residents, property managers, and support teams rely on every day. You'll own the product direction for AI-powered features - from early exploration to production - and make sure we're always solving the right problems in the right order.
   

  

 You'll drive the roadmap for tools like our case triage engine - defining what great looks like, gathering user feedback, and working with engineers to continuously improve how we understand and route incoming maintenance issues.
   

  

**Your Main Responsibilities**
* Bring structure, clarity, and momentum to a complex case management landscape shaped by decentralized organizations, differing local processes, legacy systems, and uneven technical maturity.
* Own the product direction for AI-powered case management automation, turning ambiguous operational needs into clear priorities, actionable backlog items, and scalable solutions.
* Build deep understanding of how case handling works in practice today across the business - immersing yourself in operational workflows, pain points, and variations to ensure we solve real problems in ways users trust and adopt.
* Act as the bridge between AI engineers, operations teams, domain experts, and business stakeholders - translating between technical possibilities, business needs, and day-to-day operational reality.
* Drive alignment, prioritization, and delivery together with the AI engineering team and stakeholders, balancing business value, technical constraints, data dependencies, and long-term product evolution.
* Drive adoption and measurable impact end to end - from discovery and rollout to follow-up, iteration, and continuous improvement based on usage, feedback, and outcomes.


**Who You Are**
* You thrive in complexity and ambiguity, and you are at your best when bringing structure, focus, and progress to situations that are not yet well defined.
* You are genuinely curious about how work happens in practice and willing to go deep into the domain to understand workflows, edge cases, and operational pain points.
* You build trust quickly and communicate clearly across functions, levels, and backgrounds - from highly technical teams to operational stakeholders and senior leadership.
* You are comfortable making decisions with incomplete information, balancing short-term needs with long-term direction, and adjusting course as new insights emerge.
* You combine strategic product thinking with hands-on execution, and you care as much about adoption and outcomes as you do about delivery.
* You are excited by applied AI and motivated by the challenge of turning new technology into products that create real value in complex environments.


**What We Are Looking For**
* A true product owner who is the natural go-to person for the AI product domain - perceived as an owner, not a coordinator.
* Someone who can bridge the gap between engineers who care deeply about their craft and stakeholders who care about outcomes.
* A data-driven decision maker who is comfortable making trade-offs and driving alignment across functions.
* An excellent communicator - verbal and written - who can make complex AI concepts accessible to any audience.
* A team player who thrives in a collaborative, open, and supportive environment.


**Relevant Experience**
* 5+ years as a Product Manager, Product Owner, or similar product leadership role, ideally in B2B SaaS or tech-driven services.
* Hands-on experience working with AI, ML, or data-driven product teams is a strong plus.
* Experience in PropTech or the service industry is a plus.
* Bachelor's degree in a relevant field is preferred.
* Fluent in Swedish and English.


**Location**
 Our office is located at Tulegatan 4 in Stockholm, and our AI team follows a hybrid work model with three days in the office to balance focus and collaboration.
   

  

**Sounds like you?** 
 Apply today!
   

  

 #msl

title: Resident Consultant, Sweden
description:
The Opportunity
   

  

 We are looking for an experienced Consultant to join our Professional Services team in Sweden. As a Resident Consultant, you will play a key role in helping our clients define their architectural vision, design and deploy complex environments, and manage and optimize their Nutanix-based infrastructures.
   

  

 You will work closely with customers to ensure the success of their digital transformation journey — from initial design to full implementation — while delivering exceptional value and trusted technical expertise.
   

  

 Professional Services at Nutanix
   

  

 The Nutanix Professional Services team drives customer success by ensuring smooth and impactful deployments of our solutions.
   

  

 We partner with clients throughout their software-defined data center (SDDC) journey — from strategy and architecture to design, implementation, integration, and automation.
   

  

 Our mission is to enable customers to realize the full potential of their Nutanix environment, supported by a culture of collaboration, learning, and continuous improvement.
   

  

 Your Role
   

  

* Administer, operate, and support your customer’s Nutanix and VMware environments.
* Troubleshoot and resolve infrastructure issues, develop growth plans, and generate performance reports based on customer trends.
* Lead architectural strategy initiatives in collaboration with customer architecture and engineering teams.
* Act as a trusted advisor, providing guidance on Nutanix and VMware best practices, and recommending architectural or engineering improvements.
* Partner with the Nutanix Sales Team to identify new opportunities and maintain clear communication on customer progress and project updates.
* Deliver hands-on implementation, including physical setup of servers and installation of Nutanix software (ability to lift up to 20–25 kg).


 What You’ll Bring
   

  

* A calm, professional approach and a passion for helping others succeed.
* 10+ years of experience as a virtualization or infrastructure consultant.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
* Strong expertise in VMware ESX and data center virtualization, ideally with experience in Nutanix Enterprise Cloud Platform.
* 3 to 5 years of experience in automation solutions.
* Proficiency in infrastructure as code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, Ansible, and VMware Aria.
* Experience with VDI (ideally Horizon and Citrix)
* Strong programming skills in languages like Python, Bash, and PowerShell.
* Deep understanding of storage and data protection technologies (replication, snapshots, backup, restore, etc.).
* Proven ability to analyze and resolve complex, cross-platform issues.
* Hands-on approach and commitment to delivering outstanding customer experiences.
* Comfort with leading workshops, whiteboarding sessions, and presenting to both technical and business audiences.
* Languages: Fluent in English and Swedish

title: Data Engineer - Amazon Redshift
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled
 **Data Engineer** 
 with strong expertise in
 **AWS Redshift** 
 ,
 **dbt** 
 , and cloud-based data warehouse technologies. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, optimizing, and maintaining scalable data transformation pipelines and analytical data models in a modern cloud data ecosystem.
 



 The role requires deep technical knowledge of Redshift internals, SQL performance optimization, and modern data engineering practices to support enterprise analytics and reporting solutions.
 



 Key Responsibilities
 


* Design, develop, and optimize scalable data pipelines and transformation workflows
* Build and maintain data models using
 **dbt (Data Build Tool)**
* Optimize Amazon Redshift performance through:
* Workload Management (WLM)
* Distribution and Sort Keys
* Query execution plan analysis
* Query tuning and optimization
* Develop high-performance SQL queries for analytics and reporting workloads
* Implement dimensional and normalized data models based on business requirements
* Monitor and troubleshoot Redshift cluster performance using:
* AWS CloudWatch
* Redshift Console
* Collaborate with data analysts, architects, and business teams to deliver scalable data solutions
* Support CI/CD and version-controlled development practices for dbt projects
* Ensure data quality, governance, and best practices across the data platform
* Participate in performance reviews, root cause analysis, and optimization initiatives



 Required Skills & Competencies
 



 Essential Skills
 


* 6+ years of experience in Data Engineering, preferably in cloud data warehouse environments
* Strong expertise in:
* Amazon Redshift internals
* WLM configuration
* Distribution & Sort Key optimization
* Query execution plan analysis
* SQL performance tuning
* Hands-on experience with
 **dbt** 
 for data transformation and model development
* Advanced SQL programming and optimization skills
* Strong understanding of:
* Dimensional Modeling (Kimball methodology)
* Normalized data models
* Hybrid data modeling strategies
* Experience with monitoring and observability tools:
* AWS CloudWatch
* Redshift Console
* Familiarity with:
* CI/CD pipelines
* Git/version control
* Collaborative development workflows in dbt

title: In-Country Service Delivery Manager
description:
Unilabs is implementing a Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) to centralise core IT services while retaining essential in-country capabilities aligned to regulatory, business, and operational needs.
   

  

 The In-Country Service Delivery Manager (ICSDM) is a key enabler of this operating model. The role is accountable for embedding global services into the country, completing operational run transfer, and ensuring a stable, high-quality end-to-end service for the business.
   

  

 The ICSDM acts as the single authority in-market for IT service outcomes, serving as the bridge between the GSSC, global service towers, retained local services, suppliers, and business stakeholders.
   

  

 The In-Country Service Delivery Manager is accountable for embedding the GSSC operating model within the market, ensuring stable service transition, strong local governance, and high-quality IT outcomes while acting as the bridge between global delivery and local business needs.
   

  

 Scope of Global Services (Delivered via GSSC)
   

  

 Following transformation, the GSSC will provide services across the following towers:
   

  

* Cloud & Hosting
* Networks
* End User Services
* Integration Services
* Service Management & Monitoring


 The SDM is accountable for successful transition, adoption, and ongoing alignment of these services within the country.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* GSSC Service Transition & Run Transfer
* Accountable for the end-to-end transition of services from local delivery to the GSSC model
* Acts as the primary conduit between in-country operations and global service delivery teams
* Owns local operational acceptance, ensuring services meet security, regulatory, resilience, and business readiness requirements
* Coordinates knowledge transfer, documentation, handover, and stabilisation activities
* Leads change adoption with local IT teams and business stakeholders through structured communication and training
* Service Management & Performance Oversight
* Ensures services delivered by the GSSC meet agreed SLAs, XLAs, and business outcomes
* Provides in-country oversight of incidents, service requests, changes, and escalations
* Uses service data and reporting to identify trends, risks, and improvement opportunities
* Drives continuous service improvement in partnership with global service owners
* Change & Release Management
* Governs centrally driven changes impacting the country, ensuring:
* Local impact and dependency assessment
* End-to-end risk mitigation
* Business readiness and communication
* Owns country-specific changes that interface with global services (e.g. cloud migrations, security controls)
* Ensures local knowledge is embedded into global change planning
* Incident, Major Incident & Problem Management
* Acts as in-country escalation owner for major and high-impact incidents
* Coordinates with GSSC, global towers, and suppliers to minimise business disruption
* Represents country impact during major incident calls and reviews
* Ensures recurring issues are addressed via structured problem management and root-cause remediation
* Retained Local Services & Capabilities
* Manages services that remain outside GSSC scope, including:
* Local or regulatory applications
* Legacy platforms
* Market-specific integrations
* Ensures retained services comply with global security, architecture, and operational standards
* Provides local input to global roadmaps and transformation priorities
* Supplier & Commercial Management
* Manages local suppliers, break-fix partners, and retained vendors in line with the target operating model
* Monitors performance against KPIs and contractual obligations
* Identifies opportunities for rationalisation, termination, or transition to global suppliers
* Escalates risks and disputes early with clear resolution paths
* Provides local requirements into global tenders, sourcing activities, and renewals
* Local Infrastructure & Data Centre Exit
* Supports the global strategy to exit local data centres and migrate services to cloud platforms
* Manages local suppliers for systems not yet cloud-ready, ensuring stability and compliance
* Maintains visibility of legacy risks and lifecycle constraints within the country
* In-Country Break/Fix & On-Site Support
* Manages local break-fix and on-site support arrangements
* Ensures appropriate skills coverage, training, and handover as services transition to GSSC
* Maintains user experience and operational continuity during and after change
* Leadership & Stakeholder Engagement
* Acts as the trusted IT service partner to the Market CIO and senior business stakeholders
* Communicates service performance, risks, and change impacts in clear business terms
* Leads local teams through transformation with clarity on roles and accountability
* Promotes a One-Unilabs culture across local, global, and GSSC teams


**Requirements**
* Proven experience leading IT service transition and operational transformation within multinational or shared-service environments
* Strong background in IT service management (ITIL-aligned), including incident, change, problem, and supplier management
* Experience managing transitions from local delivery models to global/shared service centres (GSSC)
* Ability to coordinate cross-functional stakeholders across local, regional, and global teams
* Strong operational governance skills, including SLA/XLA oversight, escalation management, and continuous service improvement
* Experience managing hybrid infrastructure environments, cloud migrations, and retained local services
* Demonstrated ability to lead change adoption, communication, training, and business readiness activities
* Strong vendor and commercial management capabilities, including KPI oversight and supplier performance management
* Excellent leadership, stakeholder engagement, communication, and problem-solving skills
* Analytical, resilient, and adaptable mindset within complex transformation programmes
* Fluency in English and Swidish
* IT service management or leadership certifications considered advantageous

title: Solutions Architect
description:
**Integration & Solutions Architect** 




 Location: Stockholm (primary) | Warsaw (secondary) — Hybrid
 



 Department: Digital
 



 Reports to: Digital Business Applications Manager
 



  





  





**About OX2** 




 OX2 is one of Europe’s leading developers and operators of renewable energy. With a strong track record across wind, solar, and energy storage, we operate across multiple European markets and continue to expand rapidly. As we grow, digital capabilities are central to enabling our business. We are building a scalable, modern, and intelligent digital ecosystem and this role sits at the heart of it.
 



  





 This role has a natural growth path. Depending on your interests and strengths, it can evolve toward full Enterprise Architecture, a data engineering and analytics specialism, or a hybrid of both. We will invest in the direction that fits you and the business.
 



  





  





**About the Role** 




 As Integration & Solutions Architect at OX2, you will be the go-to person for how our systems connect, how data flows across our landscape, and how we make sound architectural decisions as we scale.
 



  





 This is a hands-on role. You will spend meaningful time doing: building, configuring, and troubleshooting, not just advising. At the same time, you will bring structure to our integration landscape and help us evolve toward a more governed, future-proof architecture over time. You will work closely with Product Owners, Business Analysts, and the wider Digital team, as well as with external integration partners you help source and manage. Independence and pragmatism are just as important here as technical depth.
 



  





**Your key responsibilities will include:** 



* Own and develop OX2’s integration landscape - hands-on design, implementation, and maintenance of integrations across our core platforms (IFS, OneStream, Microsoft Fabric, SAP SuccessFactors, and others)
* Source, onboard, and manage an external integration partner to support delivery capacity - you will scope the need, run the evaluation, and govern the relationship
* Maintain and continuously improve our system landscape documentation - including system maps, data flows, and integration inventories by engaging the right stakeholders across the business
* Translate business requirements into technical solutions: from requirement specification through to ETL delivery, data pipeline design, and integration testing
* Support our data platform strateg, working alongside our Analytical Engineer on Microsoft Fabric and contributing to decisions on our data warehouse evolution
* Guide Product Owners on integration and architecture choices, ensuring solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable
* Contribute to OX2’s longer-term architectural foundation, including AI-readiness and data exposure across key platforms



  





  





**Who You Are** 




 We are looking for someone who combines genuine technical ability with business curiosity and the confidence to work with a high degree of autonomy.
 



  





**We believe you have:** 



* A relevant academic background: Computer Science, Information Systems, or similar
* 3–5 years of experience in solution architecture, integration development, or a senior technical analyst role
* Hands-on experience designing and building integrations, you have personally owned deliveries, not just reviewed them
* Experience or clear capability in sourcing and managing external technical partners or vendors
* Ability to create and maintain system landscape documentation by engaging stakeholders across functions
* Practical experience with ETL processes and data pipelines, picking up data from external sources and landing it in a target platform
* Familiarity with cloud platforms Azure (including Microsoft Fabric) and/or AWS
* Fluency in English is a must; Swedish is a strong plus



  





**As a person you are:** 



* Comfortable working independently, you do not need a large team around you to be effective
* Pragmatic and delivery-focused, you find the right solution for the context, not the theoretically perfect one
* A clear communicator who can translate between technical and business language
* Structured enough to bring order to complexity, without over-engineering



  





**We consider it a strong merit if you also have:** 



* Experience with IFS ERP or OneStream EPM
* Experience in renewable energy, infrastructure, or asset-heavy industries
* Understanding of IPP or project finance business models
* Knowledge of integration platforms and middleware (Azure Functions, API management, or similar)
* Experience supporting AI or ML initiatives at an enterprise level



  





  





**Why Join OX2?** 



* Be part of a purpose-driven company accelerating the transition to renewable energy
* A high-impact role with real ownership and visibility from day one
* An international environment across multiple European markets
* A genuine growth path, architectural, data-oriented, or both shaped around you
* Flexible hybrid setup, primarily Stockholm-based



  





  





**Recruitment Process** 




 We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible. The recruitment process includes an initial screening and introduction, interviews with the hiring manager and key stakeholders, and final discussions and offer.

title: SE - Back End Developer / Engineer III
description:
**Role : SE - Back End Developer / Engineer III** 




**Type : Contract(B2B)** 




**Mode : Onsite** 




**Location : Stockholm, Sweden** 




  





**Job Overview:** 




 We are looking for a senior Backend Engineer that will join Confidence, Client's experimentation platform, built on the same testing infrastructure that powers product decisions across Client and is now offered to external product teams. Confidence currently has external customers and have been built with design partners in a closed beta for the last two years.
 



 WHAT YOU'LL DO:
 


* Design, build, and run scalable Java services that power our merchandising platform and support conversion and upsell journeys
* Work closely with your squad to design backend systems, APIs, and data models on Google Cloud Platform
* Partner with product managers and engineers to solve meaningful technical challenges
* Improve and scale data pipelines that support experimentation, heuristics, and machine learning applications
* Contribute to architecture decisions that improve reliability, performance, and long-term maintainability
* Support production systems end-to-end, ensuring services are observable, reliable, and continuously improving
* Share knowledge through code reviews and collaboration, helping strengthen the team’s technical practices
* Work from our office in Stockholm



  





 WHO YOU ARE:
 


* You have 5+ years of experience building backend systems using Java
* You have strong computer science fundamentals and experience working with distributed systems at scale
* You have experience designing and operating cloud-native services (e.g., GCP or AWS)
* You are comfortable designing APIs and working with partners to translate needs into scalable solutions
* You have experience running high-volume services in production and understand reliability, observability, and performance
* You collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and take ownership of complex work from idea to delivery
* You seek feedback, learn continuously, and look for ways to improve systems and how teams work together
* You have experience with technologies such as gRPC and Bigtable
* You have experience working with data warehouses such as Snowflake, BigQuery or Databricks

title: Channel Manager - Reader portfolio      .
description:
Channel Manager - Reader portfolio
   

  

 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

 32 - 38 hrs
   

  

 What are you going to do?
   

  

 As
 **Channel Manager for Nordics, Baltics, Central & Eastern Europe** 
 , you are responsible for driving the growth of Nedap’s Reader portfolio across the region. You build strong relationships with OEMs, distributors, and system integrators, helping them create secure, future-proof access management solutions.
   

  

 Our Reader portfolio plays a key role in Nedap’s Full Access Management strategy within Security. With a strong position in long-range identification, RFID, vehicle identification, and multi-technology reader solutions, we support organizations worldwide in securing people, buildings, and assets. As part of Nedap in Security, the Reader portfolio further strengthens our position in the growing access management market and creates new opportunities to combine hardware, software, and cloud-based services into one integrated solution.
   

  

 You own the regional channel pipeline and translate market opportunities into sustainable growth. Together with our partners, you identify new opportunities, support deal execution, and strengthen Nedap’s position within the access control ecosystem.
   

  

 At Nedap, we believe technology starts with people. That means you are not only focused on commercial success, but also on creating long-term value for customers and partners. You work closely with colleagues from product management, marketing, and technical support to deliver solutions that truly make an impact.
   

  

 As part of Nedap in Security, you contribute to our ambition to strengthen our leading position in access management through innovative Digital Twin Technology solutions.
   

  

 Start your application
   

  

 Your responsibilities
   

  

 Develop and execute the regional channel strategy for the Reader portfolio across Nordics, Baltics, and Central & Eastern Europe
   

  

 Build and strengthen relationships with OEMs, distributors, and system integrators
   

  

 Manage and grow the regional sales pipeline, from opportunity identification to deal closure
   

  

 Support partners during commercial and technical sales processes
   

  

 Identify market trends, customer needs, and growth opportunities within the access control ecosystem
   

  

 Work closely with product management, marketing, and technical support teams
   

  

 Represent Nedap at customer meetings, partner events, and industry exhibitions
   

  

 Contribute to the continued growth and positioning of Nedap in Security
   

  

 Your team
   

  

 You become part of Nedap in Security, one of Nedap’s four key markets. Our Security business unit develops access management solutions that help organizations worldwide secure people, buildings, and assets. We combine decades of expertise in access control with innovative cloud and Digital Twin Technology solutions.
   

  

 Within the team, entrepreneurship and ownership are central. You work in an international environment with colleagues who combine technical expertise with a strong commercial mindset. Together, we continuously improve our solutions and strengthen our market position.
   

  

 Our offer
   

  

 What's in it for you?
   

  

 At Nedap, we offer more than just a job. You become part of a people-first organization where you get the freedom and responsibility to make an impact.
   

  

 You can expect:
   

  

* An international role with a high level of autonomy and ownership
* The opportunity to work with innovative access management technologies
* A collaborative and entrepreneurial work environment
* Plenty of room for personal and professional growth
* A competitive salary and attractive secondary benefits
* A hybrid way of working with flexibility and trust


 All terms of employment
   

  

**Required Skills**
 You are a commercially driven professional with extensive experience in security and access management. You know how to build strong relationships with channel partners and customers, manage complex sales processes, and identify growth opportunities in an international market.
   

  

* Minimum of 10 years of experience in B2B sales or channel management within security, access control, or related technology markets
* Experience working with OEMs, distributors, or system integrators
* Strong network within the regional physical security market
* Entrepreneurial, proactive, and independent way of working
* Affinity with technology and access management solutions
* Excellent communication skills in English; additional regional languages are a plus
* Willingness to travel regularly within the region
* Preferably based in the Nordics


 Get in touch!
   

  

 Interested in the vacancy
 **Channel Manager - Reader portfolio** 
 or curious about what it’s like to work at Nedap? Feel free to contact Paulette Kamperman via paulette.kamperman@nedap.com. We’re happy to help!

title: Sales Development Representative - Dutch speaking
description:
**Be a Key Player in the Future of Telematics – Apply as a Sales Development Representative at ABAX!**
 Do you have a talent for building relationships, love talking to people, and enjoy opening doors to new opportunities? Then we have the perfect role for you!
   

  

 Our Stockholm office is expanding – and we’re now looking for an ambitious, results-driven
 **Sales Development Representative** 
 to strengthen our growth. In this role, you’ll
 **focus on the Dutch markets,** 
 so we’re looking for someone fluent in
 **Dutch or Flemish** 
 . You may already have experience in sales, or perhaps you’re at the start of your career and eager to develop in a role with great potential. With us, you’ll have the chance to grow alongside a successful team in a fast-moving and future-focused organisation.
   

  

**What does the role involve?**
 As a Sales Development Representative at ABAX, you’ll be the crucial first point of contact for our future customers. You’ll be the face of the company and play a central role in our sales journey. Your mission? To book high-quality meetings for our Account Executives, laying the foundation for strong customer relationships.
   

  

 You’ll be part of a passionate team where we support one another, celebrate successes, and work towards shared goals.
   

  

**Your responsibilities:**
* Prospect and identify new business opportunities – with the phone as your main tool.
* Book relevant meetings with the right decision-makers.
* Work closely with the sales team – you’re a key player in creating a first-class customer journey from the very first contact.
* Build your pipeline and work strategically to hit your targets.
* Work smart! Use our CRM tools, reporting systems, and training platform to work more effectively and achieve results faster. We give you the tools – you create the results!
* Drive our growth by booking meetings that lead to new customers.


**Who are you?**
 You’re an energetic person who sees opportunities where others see obstacles. You’re motivated by results, enjoy challenges, and are always looking to develop.
   

  

**We believe you:**
* Have experience in proactive sales or eager to learn.
* We are goal-oriented, structured, and self-driven.
* Communicate fluently in both Dutch and/or Flemish and English.
* Enjoy working in a team, but are also confident in taking the initiative.
* Identify with our values: enthusiastic, customer-focused, innovative, and sustainable.


 Do you also have experience in SaaS, telematics, leasing, service sales, or similar? That’s a big plus – but not a requirement.
   

  

**What do we offer you?**
 At ABAX, you don’t just get a job – you get a career opportunity. Here, we value collaboration, innovation, and a joyful workplace.
   

  

 We offer:
   

  

* Comprehensive onboarding through ABAX School
* Competitive salary + bonus scheme
* Private healthcare and insurance
* An inspiring work environment in our modern office in Stockholm


 You’ll be working in a fast-growing international company – where no day is the same, and where your ideas and energy make a real difference.
   

  

**Background Checks & References:**
 Employment at ABAX depends on receiving satisfactory references.
   

  

 As part of our recruitment process, we carry out digital reference checks via RefApp, an online solution.
   

  

 Please note that a background check may also be conducted alongside the reference check.
   

  

**Equal Opportunities:**
 At ABAX, we are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for all our colleagues. This commitment also extends to ensuring our recruitment routines and processes are as inclusive as possible. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to join us and help strengthen our focus on equality and diversity in the workplace.

title: Senior Regulatory Counsel
description:
* Build your future with Sovos.


 If you're seeking a career where innovation meets impact, you've come to the right place. As a global leader, Sovos is transforming tax compliance from a business requirement to a force for growth while revolutionising how businesses navigate the ever-changing regulatory landscape.
   

  

 At Sovos, we're dedicated to more than just solving compliance challenges -- we're committed to making a positive and lasting difference in everything we do. Our teams operate on the modern edge of digital technology, working not only to solve complex business challenges but also to enrich our personal, professional, and local communities.
   

  

 Our purpose-built systems provide the tools you need to thrive in a world where governments demand increased visibility, faster reporting and greater control over business processes. Excited about the possibilities? So are we!
   

  

 Don't worry if you don't check all the boxes -- apply anyway! We're focused on hiring the right people, not just the "right" resume. It's not about what you've done elsewhere; it's all about what you're capable of doing here.
   

  

**The Work You'll Do**
 The EMEA Regulatory Analysis & Design team based in Portugal, Sweden, and Germany, offers interesting and challenging research work in a multinational environment within the cutting-edge and technology-driven fiscal compliance space. Our team is truly global. While physically located in a handful of countries, we have roots in places such as Albania, Chile, Brazil, Bulgaria, Greece, Italy, Mexico, Romania, Sweden, and the United States. Together, we provide the regulatory intelligence that powers our suite of products.
   

  

 Our work within the EMEA team includes global regulatory research relating to electronic invoicing and tax reporting as well as periodic VAT filing. The subject matter of your research will not only include standard regulatory changes but also involves tracking technical changes and information security updates. In short, our job is to identify, track, and analyze governmentally imposed changes so that they are accounted for in our solution before they take effect. The Regulatory Analysis team works closely with Product Management to ensure that our products meet relevant legal requirements. In essence, we co-design our solutions with our colleagues in Product and Technology. If you are interested in the intersection of tax and technology, this is the place for you.
   

  

 As a Senior Regulatory Counsel, you will be tasked with the research and monitoring work described above for your assigned jurisdictions as well as reviewing the work of junior team members for accuracy and completeness. As a senior team member you will carry research responsibilities for our highest profile initiatives and will be expected to independently support customer-facing, cross-functional, and thought leadership functions. We are an AI-enabled company, so you should be comfortable using such tools as a critical part of your research process.
   

  

**More Specifically, You Will**
* Proactively and continually monitor legislative and regulatory developments concerning electronic invoicing, digital reporting, and related fiscal obligations across supported jurisdictions.
* As a senior member of the team, you will be expected to help shape research priorities, mentor junior team members, and contribute to the strategic direction of the Regulatory Analysis function.
* Develop an understanding of the technology behind our solutions to provide meaningful insight on the impact of new and evolving legal requirements to our existing offerings.
* Explain complex legal requirements in clear language for internal and external audiences (e.g., workshops, direct customer inquiries/conference calls, webinars, blogs, and speaking engagements.)
* Robustly support Sovos colleagues directly involved in designing, building, implementing, supporting, and maintaining Sovos solutions.
* Develop a strong working knowledge of Sovos' Regulatory Affairs function and support the team's engagement with public authorities as needed.
* Work continuously with Sovos Alliances, Sales Engineering, Product Management, and other relevant functions to develop and maintain a robust understanding of Sovos’ ecosystem and the specific business processes and associated compliance needs that arise in that context.
* In collaboration with Sovos Marketing and partner management functions, participate as a Sovos regulatory thought leader and subject matter expert in webinars, advisory boards, external speaking engagements and publications.


**What We Need From You**
* Juris Doctor (JD) degree or equivalent master’s degree in law, finance, or accounting.
* 4+ years of experience analyzing and understanding global eInvoicing and digital reporting requirements.
* Experience working for a technology provider in the eInvoicing space is strongly preferred.
* Proficiency in legislative tracking and tax research techniques.
* Excellent research and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret tax legislation and regulatory guidance.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex legal and technical information for both specialist and non-specialist audiences.
* Ability to interpret and understand technical specifications set by tax authorities and their relationship with compliance and business processes.
* Strong team orientation with a history of working collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
* Self-motivated with a strong drive to succeed.
* Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and prioritize activities.
* Excellent command of spoken and written English.
* Additional language skills such as Chinese, French, German, Hungarian, Italian, Arabic, Spanish, or Polish is a plus.


**What Does Sovos Offer You?**
 The tools to enhance your life - because we want you to enjoy your life outside of work and inside!
   

  

 Sovos is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing an environment that celebrates diversity and where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and employees. We do not discriminate against race, color, religions, national origin, age, sex, marital status, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic provided by law. At Sovos, all employees are encouraged to bring their whole selves to work.
   

  

**Company Background**
 Sovos is a global provider of tax, compliance and trust solutions and services that enable businesses to navigate an increasingly regulated world with true confidence. Purpose-built for always-on compliance capabilities, our scalable IT-driven solutions meet the demands of an evolving and complex global regulatory landscape. Sovos' cloud-based software platform provides an unparalleled level of integration with business applications and government compliance processes.
   

  

 More than 100,000 customers in 100+ countries -- including half the Fortune 500 -- trust Sovos for their compliance needs. Sovos annually processes more than three billion transactions across 19,000 global tax jurisdictions. Bolstered by a robust partner program more than 400 strong, Sovos brings to bear an unrivaled global network for companies across industries and geographies. Founded in 1979, Sovos has operations across the Americas and Europe, and is owned by Hg and TA Associates. For more information visit http://www.sovos.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Twitter.

 If you're seeking a career where innovation meets impact, you've come to the right place. As a global leader, Sovos is transforming tax compliance from a business requirement to a force for growth while revolutionising how businesses navigate the ever-changing regulatory landscape.

**What Does Sovos Offer You?**

 Sovos is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing an environment that celebrates diversity and where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and employees. We do not discriminate against race, color, religions, national origin, age, sex, marital status, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic provided by law.
